• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

Dynamic Network Exploration

Exploratory Data Analysis of the Temporal Direct Network Data

We are running exploratory data analyses of the French transfer networks. OUr data comes from the direct transfers of all France hospitals in 2024, all within a 7-day time frame aggregation within one year. Therefore, our first analysis would be a descriptive analyses of the basic network characteristics.

Weekly Transfer Window Data

Let’s explore the direct transfers with 7-day (weekly) time window,starting for all year timespan.


# Explore the data structure
# Let's make sure that the date span is from January to December.

head(weekly)
tail(weekly)

#Assigning seasons to the data

df <- weekly %>%
  mutate(
    window_start = as.Date(window_start),
    month = month(window_start),
    season = case_when(
      month %in% c(12, 1, 2)  ~ "Winter",
      month %in% c(3, 4, 5)   ~ "Spring",
      month %in% c(6, 7, 8)   ~ "Summer",
      month %in% c(9, 10, 11) ~ "Autumn"
    )
  )

# Creating the summary statistics of what i wanted to know about the network over time
# I want to know the Number of connections (edges), hospitals (nodes), 
# the number of total transfers between all hospitals within that week
# the mean and median number of transfers between hospitals
# the minimum and max number of transfers between hospitals
# 
# Group it with the Weekly Window Number, the Start Date of the Window

#FIrst, getting the overall for the year.

# Number of sending hospitals
num_sender = length(unique(df$finessGeo_origin))
num_sender
[1] 1427
# Number of receiver hospitals
num_receiver = length(unique(df$finessGeo_target))
num_receiver
[1] 1422
#Total number of transfers in the year
num_transfers_allyear = sum(df$weight)
num_transfers_allyear
[1] 4325040
#Max 10 transfers between pairs of hospitals within a week period
df %>%  slice_max(weight, n=5)
NA

Temporal Analysis of Trends by Weekly Time Window

Looking at the averages and summary statistics trend for day-to-day trends to see the ups and downs of the number of nodes, edges, transfers, and otehers

[[1]]

[[2]]

[[3]]

[[4]]

[[5]]

[[6]]

Calculate the centrality measures statistics for the network data


# Define a function to calculate network-level statistics
calculate_network_metrics <- function(df) {
  
  # Initialize an empty list to store the results
  results <- list()
  
  # Loop over each unique time window
  for(t in unique(df$window_start)) {
    
    # Filter data for the current time window
    time_df <- df %>% filter(window_start == t)
    
    # Create a directed graph for the current time window with edge weights
    g <- graph_from_data_frame(time_df, directed = TRUE, vertices = NULL)
    E(g)$weight <- time_df$weight  # Add the weight attribute to the edges
    
    # Calculate network-level metrics
    network_stats <- data.frame(
      time_window = t,
      num_nodes = vcount(g),
      num_edges = ecount(g),
      avg_degree = mean(degree(g)),
      density = edge_density(g),
      avg_path_length = tryCatch({
        # Compute shortest path lengths (weighted)
        sp_lengths <- shortest.paths(g, mode = "all", weights = E(g)$weight)
        # Remove infinite distances (no path found)
        sp_lengths[sp_lengths == Inf] <- NA
        mean(sp_lengths, na.rm = TRUE)
      }, error = function(e) NA),
      clustering_coef = tryCatch({
        # Compute local clustering coefficient for weighted directed graph
        transitivity(g, type = "local", weights = E(g)$weight)
      }, error = function(e) NA),
      num_connected_components = components(g)$no
    )
    
    # Store the results
    results[[as.character(t)]] <- network_stats
  }
  
  # Combine results for all time windows into one dataframe
  final_results <- bind_rows(results)
  
  return(final_results)
}

# Run the function and get the network-level statistics
network_metrics_df <- calculate_network_metrics(df)
View(network_metrics_df)
network_metrics_df

#Export the dataset as result
#
# Make sure the folder exists
if(!dir.exists("./Outputs/")) dir.create("./Outputs/")
write.csv(network_metrics_df, file = "./Outputs/network_metrics_direct_weekly_2024.csv")

# Define a function to calculate network-level statistics
calculate_network_metrics <- function(df) {
  
  # Initialize an empty list to store the results
  results <- list()
  
  # Loop over each unique time window
  for(t in unique(df$window_start)) {
    
    # Filter data for the current time window
    time_df <- df %>% filter(window_start == t)
    
    # Create a directed graph for the current time window with edge weights
    g <- graph_from_data_frame(time_df, directed = TRUE, vertices = NULL)
    E(g)$weight <- time_df$weight  # Add the weight attribute to the edges
    
    # Calculate network-level metrics
    network_stats <- data.frame(
      time_window = t,
      num_nodes = vcount(g),
      num_edges = ecount(g),
      avg_degree = mean(degree(g)),
      density = edge_density(g),
      avg_path_length = tryCatch({
        # Compute shortest path lengths (weighted)
        sp_lengths <- shortest.paths(g, mode = "all", weights = E(g)$weight)
        # Remove infinite distances (no path found)
        sp_lengths[sp_lengths == Inf] <- NA
        mean(sp_lengths, na.rm = TRUE)
      }, error = function(e) NA),
      clustering_coef = tryCatch({
        # Compute local clustering coefficient for weighted directed graph
        transitivity(g, type = "local", weights = E(g)$weight)
      }, error = function(e) NA),
      num_connected_components = components(g)$no
    )
    
    # Store the results
    results[[as.character(t)]] <- network_stats
  }
  
  # Combine results for all time windows into one dataframe
  final_results <- bind_rows(results)
  
  return(final_results)
}

# Run the function and get the network-level statistics
network_metrics_df <- calculate_network_metrics(df)
network_metrics_df

#Export the dataset as result
#
# Make sure the folder exists
if(!dir.exists("./Outputs/")) dir.create("./Outputs/")
write.csv(network_metrics_df, file = "./Outputs/network_metrics_direct_weekly_2
